description: Suppressing a SQL compiler/linter diagnostic without fixing the underlying contract hides a real defect.
body: Failure mode: a SQL diagnostic (compiler error, type/lint warning) is silenced with a suppression directive or cast instead of fixing the cause, so the underlying contract violation ships. Detection signals: new suppression annotations or ignore directives appear near the change; the warning count drops without a logic fix; the same class of error recurs. Do not suppress a SQL diagnostic to make the gate pass. Safer fallback: fix the contract the diagnostic points to, or, if suppression is genuinely correct, record why with evidence and keep it narrowly scoped.
